title: Regex validation scans backwards to avoid false-positive on header names
description: lib.rs:170-195: validate_stmt validates only the last Str/StringList in a :regex statement as a regex pattern, not the first (header field name). A reviewer might suggest validating all strings; this is wrong. 'X[Special]' is a legal RFC 5322 header name but an invalid regex. The backwards-scan approach is the correct way to identify the key-list without knowing the schema of each Sieve command. The test compile_regex_does_not_validate_header_name_as_pattern verifies this property explicitly. Defend the backwards-scan approach.
